text.title: azure-front-door Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-front-door as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Front Door + App Gateway chained without Private Link sends traffic over the public internet between them — the docs imply but do not enforce private chaining.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the `private_link` block on the Front Door origin, the App Gateway public IP is used as the origin. This means traffic is not on the Azure backbone and the App Gateway must remain publicly accessible.

failure-mode.description: Front Door rules engine syntax differs from App Gateway URL Path Map; agents copy routing rules between them and break routing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Front Door uses `cdn_frontdoor_route` with `patterns_to_match` arrays; App Gateway uses `url_path_map` with `path_rule` blocks. They have different wildcard syntax and matching semantics.

failure-mode.description: Agents configure dev/test environments with Front Door and are surprised by 5-15 minute propagation delays after every config change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Front Door config changes propagate globally to all PoPs. Use App Gateway alone in dev/test for faster iteration. Reserve Front Door for staging and production.
